title: [hatterene] Battle Guide
summary: A [psychic]/[fairy] Trick Room wallbreaker that bounces hazards with [magic-bounce] while swinging 136 Special Attack on the slow side.
strengths: [magic-bounce] reflects stealth-rock, taunt, and status automatically
Base 136 SpA with [psychic]/[fairy] STAB covers Dark, Dragon, and Fighting
Base 29 Speed becomes top tier under [trick-room]
weaknesses: Stacked Steel, Poison, and Ghost 2x weaknesses
57/95 physical bulk folds to knock-off and u-turn
Loses all tempo the second Trick Room expires

recommendedItems.reason: Blocks taunt and encore on the Trick Room setup turn

bodyMarkdown:
## Overview
[hatterene] is the [psychic]/[fairy] piece that anchors Trick Room teams in Champions MA. The 57/95/136 offensive frame already hits hard, but the real value comes from [magic-bounce] — every stealth-rock, taunt, spore, and will-o-wisp the opponent throws comes right back at them for free.

## Strengths
136 SpA is still premium for doubles, and the [psychic]/[fairy] STAB combo cleanly threatens Dark, Dragon, Fighting, and Poison at once. Base 29 Speed reads like a liability but becomes the top of the Speed tier under [trick-room], which is why the [quiet] nature drops it even further. [magic-bounce] alone forces hazard leads to rethink turn one, which is a tempo win before the first move is clicked.

## Weaknesses & Answers
The 2x weaknesses to Steel, Poison, and Ghost pile up fast. Gholdengo, Corviknight, and Metagross-style walls flip the matchup, and any knock-off or u-turn user chips through the 57 HP before Trick Room even goes up. Once Trick Room expires [hatterene]'s 29 Speed is exposed and priority Ghost attackers clean up.

## Recommended Moveset
[psychic] is the locked STAB for its raw numbers, and [dazzling-gleam] is the spread [fairy] STAB that doubles teams want. [mystical-fire] is mandatory coverage — without it Gholdengo and Metagross wall the set completely. The fourth slot is [trick-room] itself, because [hatterene] is both the setter and the main abuser. In singles, swap [dazzling-gleam] for psyshock to break specially defensive walls.

## Item · Ability · Tera
[mental-herb] is the premium pick: on the Trick Room setup turn it blocks taunt and encore, which is exactly when [hatterene] is most vulnerable. [leftovers] is the alternative for longer games where [magic-bounce] already does the turn-one work. The ability is locked to [magic-bounce]. Tera [fairy] cranks [dazzling-gleam], while Tera [water] erases Steel and Poison weaknesses and turns [hatterene] into a genuine wall.

## Teammates & Synergy
[hatterene] wants heavy slow wallbreakers behind it — anything that abuses inverted Speed. Fighting and Ground partners clear Steel answers like Gholdengo, a [fairy]-resistant pivot covers Poison threats, and an imprison or taunt user punishes opposing Trick Room mirrors. A priority-denying [fairy] or Fighting ally keeps Ghost sucker-punch lines from stealing kills.

## Play Patterns
Lead [hatterene], click [trick-room] turn one, and start swinging 136 SpA attacks from turn two. Because [magic-bounce] is live, never switch out of a hazard lead — bait their click and reflect it. Save Tera for the turn a Steel or Ghost move is guaranteed so the swap to [water] or [fairy] flips the matchup. Do not click [psychic] into known Dark pivots; go [dazzling-gleam] for the safer line.
